sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Microbial contamination prompts Neogen recall of pet wound solution
feed.summary: Neogen Corporation has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots within expiry of its Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution for dogs, cats, and horses. The recall stems from microbial contamination found in certain lots of the 10 mL/50 mg product vials, which has led to adverse event reports in horses when used intraarticularly, inconsistent with its labeled use. While no adverse events have been reported for labeled topical use, the company is also recalling 2 mL/20 mg vials out of caution and is working with the FDA.

feed.title: Tesla beats Q4 expectations, gives upbeat outlook on robotaxi, Optimus - update (TSLA:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Tesla exceeded Q4 earnings expectations, with its top and bottom lines beating forecasts, despite a decline in quarterly revenue. The company provided an optimistic outlook for its Cybercab and robotaxi initiatives, as well as its Optimus bot, which helped to overshadow the revenue dip. Analysts noted softening fundamentals but acknowledged that future growth from these new technologies could drive the stock higher.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) Stock Price Up 1.3% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) shares saw a 1.3% increase in trading, closing at $108.2510 with higher-than-average volume. This rise follows several analyst upgrades and price target increases from firms like Wells Fargo, Morgan Stanley, UBS, Goldman Sachs, and Bank of America. The company also recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.85 per share and reported insider stock sales.

feed.title: Ameris Bancorp: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Ameris Bancorp reported Q4 earnings of $108.4 million, or $1.59 per share, exceeding Wall Street expectations. While the bank's earnings surpassed analysts' estimates, its revenue net of interest expense of $308.1 million missed Street forecasts. For the full year, Ameris Bancorp posted a profit of $412.2 million on revenue of $1.21 billion.

feed.title: Primis Financial: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Primis Financial Corp. (FRST) reported fourth-quarter earnings of $29.5 million, or $1.20 per share. Adjusted for non-recurring gains, earnings were 10 cents per share, with revenue reaching $67.5 million. For the full year, the company posted a profit of $61.4 million, or $2.49 per share, on revenue of $188 million.

feed.title: Insider Selling: CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) CAO Sells 66,667 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: CSX Corporation's Chief Accounting Officer, Diana Sorfleet, recently sold 66,667 shares of the company's stock for approximately $2.50 million, reducing her stake by over 28%. This insider selling occurred shortly after CSX missed its quarterly earnings and revenue expectations. Despite the insider sale and missed earnings, CSX stock traded up slightly, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $39.42.

feed.title: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) Shares Down 1.4% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) saw its shares drop 1.4% on Thursday, with increased trading volume. Despite this, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a target price of $164.44, supported by recent strong earnings and revenue growth. However, the stock is considered highly valued with a P/E ratio of 56.3, and insiders have engaged in significant selling of shares.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) CEO Sells $7,959,467.70 in Stock
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) CEO Steve Sanghi recently sold 98,814 shares of the company's stock for a total of $7,959,467.70. This transaction, disclosed in an SEC filing, reduced his position by 0.99%. The article also details recent stock performance, financial metrics, institutional trading activity, and analyst ratings for Microchip Technology.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Stryker Q4 2025 earnings beat forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: Stryker (SYK) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations with an EPS of $4.47 and revenue of $7.2 billion, driven by the impressive performance of its Mako robotic system and strong sales growth across divisions. Despite beating forecasts and providing an optimistic 2026 outlook, the stock saw a slight dip in after-hours trading. The company anticipates continued high-end medtech growth in 2026, alongside efforts to manage tariff impacts and explore M&A opportunities.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical (EMN) Misses Revenue Estimates, Shares Fall
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical (EMN) experienced a 2.2% drop in shares after reporting fourth-quarter revenue of $1.97 billion, which fell short of the anticipated $2.02 billion due to reduced volumes. Despite missing revenue estimates, the company exceeded earnings per share expectations, posting adjusted EPS of $0.75 against a forecast of $0.72. Eastman Chemical's strategic focus on high-margin specialty products and a strong profitability profile are highlighted, alongside risks like market volatility and capital utilization inefficiencies.

feed.title: FIS Announces Dividend Payout for March 2026
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (FIS) has announced a dividend payout scheduled for March 24, 2026, for shareholders of record by March 10, 2026. Despite a market capitalization of $28.59 billion and diverse service offerings, the company faces financial challenges, including a low Altman Z-Score of 0.29 (indicating distress) and issues with profitability and liquidity. Valuation metrics like a high P/E ratio and a low ROIC further suggest caution for investors.

feed.title: Final Trade: Materials, SLB, URI, MSFT
feed.summary: This article features a video segment from CNBC's "Fast Money" traders discussing their final trades of the day, specifically highlighting Materials, SLB, URI, and MSFT. The content primarily points to a video discussing these stock picks and also features other recent CNBC video clips related to tech earnings and AI.

feed.title: Google stock (GOOG) flat after hours as Alphabet heads into earnings week, new cloud security deal in focus
feed.summary: Alphabet's (GOOG) stock remained flat after hours as the company approaches its earnings report on February 4th. Investors are closely scrutinizing whether significant AI investments are translating into revenue growth. Amid these financial discussions, a new $400 million cloud security partnership between PwC and Google Cloud, along with a $135 million legal settlement regarding Android data collection, are also drawing attention.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Deckers Outdoor Q3 2026 sees strong EPS beat
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corporation reported a strong Q3 2026 performance, significantly beating EPS and revenue forecasts, leading to a positive market reaction. The company's HOKA and UGG brands were key growth drivers, achieving record quarterly revenues and contributing to an increased full-year revenue guidance. Deckers highlighted strategic investments, effective marketplace management, and strong direct-to-consumer performance as factors contributing to its robust financial health and optimistic outlook for continued growth.

feed.title: Teradyne (TER) to Lead Newly Formed Joint Venture with MultiLane
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) is forming a joint venture named MultiLane Test Products (MLTP) with MultiLane to develop advanced high-speed data link testing solutions for the AI data center equipment sector. Teradyne will hold majority ownership, contributing to its strong financial health despite recent revenue challenges. The stock is trading near historical highs with significant insider selling, and high volatility, suggesting investors should be cautious despite its strategic initiatives.

feed.title: GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) Stock Price Up 2.1% - Here's Why
feed.summary: GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) shares rose 2.1% due to bullish analyst moves from JPMorgan, UBS, Goldman, and Wolfe, coupled with Citi adding GE to its high-conviction list and a consensus target near $319. The company's quarterly results surpassed expectations with $1.57 EPS and $11.9B revenue, and it provided strong FY-2026 guidance of $7.10–$7.40 EPS. Additionally, GE and NASA's successful validation of a hybrid-electric narrowbody engine further solidified GE's position in lower-emissions propulsion and future aircraft markets.

feed.title: Shareholders Approve Kimberly-Clark’s $40B Acquisition of Tylenol Maker Kenvue
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark and Kenvue shareholders have overwhelmingly approved proposals for Kimberly-Clark's $40 billion acquisition of Tylenol maker Kenvue. Kimberly-Clark CEO Mike Hsu highlighted the merger's potential to create a global health and wellness leader, while Kenvue CEO Kirk Perry emphasized accelerated innovation and expanded brand access. The transaction is expected to finalize in the second half of 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Lifts Opinion On Shares Of C.h. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. To Hold From Sell
feed.summary: CFRA has upgraded its opinion on shares of C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. from Sell to Hold. This announcement comes alongside Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, which reported a 35% year-over-year revenue increase to $609 million, surpassing analyst estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Assurant (AIZ) Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.88 a Share, Payable on March 30 to Shareholders of Record on Feb. 17
feed.summary: Assurant (AIZ) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.88 per share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on March 30, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of February 17, 2026. This news comes amidst other recent company activities including board changes and product launches.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Sets New 52-Week Low - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) recently hit a new 52-week low of $250.35, driven by analyst downgrades despite solid quarterly earnings and a $600 million share buyback authorization. While insider buying signals confidence, institutional ownership is high and some analysts have reduced their price targets. Investors might consider the mixed signals and current "Reduce" consensus rating with an average target of $313.00.

feed.title: Amazon in Talks to Invest $50 Billion in OpenAI, Expand Ties
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is reportedly in discussions to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, aiming to further expand the AI startup's existing agreement for computing power. OpenAI, known for ChatGPT, has also held talks with other major investors such as Nvidia Corp. and SoftBank Group Corp. for a new funding round. The deal is not yet finalized and terms are subject to change.

feed.title: Tyson Foods agrees to $48 million settlement with pork purchasers in price fixing case
feed.summary: Tyson Foods has reached a preliminary $48 million settlement with commercial and industrial pork purchasers in a class-action price-fixing lawsuit. This agreement is part of a larger ongoing lawsuit against multiple protein companies and adds to over $114 million in total monetary relief, including settlements from other defendants like JBS Foods and Smithfield Foods. The lawsuit, originally filed in 2018, accused Tyson and other meat producers of conspiring to control pork supply and prices.

feed.title: Coterra Energy and Devon Energy in advanced talks to merge, source says
feed.summary: Coterra Energy (CTRA) and Devon Energy are reportedly in advanced discussions to merge, with a deal possibly announced as early as next week. The potential merger, which sources suggest could form a $60 billion US shale group, has been reported by Financial Times and Bloomberg. This news follows recent adjustments in analyst price targets for Coterra Energy and activist investor Kimmeridge's plan to nominate Scott Sheffield to Coterra's board.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Board Approves CEO Charles Scharf’s 2025 Compensation
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's board has approved a $40 million compensation package for CEO Charles Scharf in 2025. This decision acknowledges his leadership in resolving regulatory issues like closing seven consent orders and removing the Federal Reserve's asset cap, improving financial metrics, and delivering higher shareholder returns. While Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates WFC as Neutral due to some financial tempering factors, the board's decision highlights significant progress under Scharf's tenure.

feed.title: Ameris Bancorp (NYSE:ABCB) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings
feed.summary: Ameris Bancorp (ABCB) reported its Q4 CY2025 earnings, with revenue slightly missing analyst estimates at $307.1 million, despite a 5.5% year-on-year increase. The company's non-GAAP profit of $1.59 per share, net interest income, net interest margin, efficiency ratio, and adjusted EPS all beat analyst expectations. CEO Palmer Proctor highlighted the strong financial performance, including a 1.54% ROA, margin expansion, positive operating leverage, and significant growth in tangible book value, positioning the company for continued growth in the Southeast.

feed.title: Moderna Collaborates With Recordati to Advance, Commercialize Investigational Therapy to Treat Metabolic Disorder
feed.summary: Moderna has announced a collaboration with Recordati to further develop and commercialize an investigational therapy aimed at treating a metabolic disorder. This partnership marks a step forward for the therapy, combining Moderna's innovative research with Recordati's commercialization expertise. The details of the metabolic disorder or the specific therapy were not disclosed in this initial announcement.

feed.title: EQT Corp expects $114 million derivative gain for fourth quarter
feed.summary: EQT Corp expects a derivative gain of $114 million for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, with net cash settlements of $35 million from derivatives. The natural gas company anticipates $44 million in net cash settlements from NYMEX natural gas hedges and $9 million in net cash settlements paid for basis and liquids hedge positions. These figures are preliminary, and final results will be detailed in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K.

feed.title: Sandisk, Western Digital beat expectations as cloud and AI data center storage demand accelerates
feed.summary: Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp. both reported better-than-expected quarterly results, driven by increasing demand for storage from cloud and AI data centers. Sandisk saw significant growth in enterprise SSDs, with its datacenter revenue up 76% year-over-year, while Western Digital also posted strong revenue growth and gross margin expansion, particularly in its cloud segment. Both companies provided optimistic outlooks for the next quarter, projecting earnings and revenue well above analyst forecasts due to strong market demand and improved pricing.

feed.title: Why your Iowa tap water looks cloudy in winter, according to the utility
feed.summary: Iowa American Water explains that cloudy or milky-looking tap water, a common winter inquiry, is caused by dissolved air (oxygen) releasing as tiny bubbles when pressure is reduced at the faucet. This phenomenon is not a health concern and the water meets all applicable regulatory standards. Customers can observe the bubbles clearing by holding a glass to light and watching the water clear from the bottom up, typically within one to two minutes.

feed.title: A Water Quality Message for Iowa American Water Customers – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Iowa American Water is addressing common customer inquiries about cloudy or milky-looking tap water, explaining that it is caused by tiny air bubbles (dissolved oxygen) and is not a health concern. This phenomenon is most noticeable in colder months because cold water absorbs more air. The company assures customers that their water meets all health regulations and provides contact information for further questions.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group completes $400 million subordinated notes offering
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) has completed a $400 million offering of 5.299% Fixed-Reset Subordinated Notes due 2036. The proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including potential share repurchases, dividends, and debt reduction. This follows strong Q4 2025 results, which led to multiple analyst price target increases and maintained Buy/Outperform ratings.

feed.title: DXC’s (NYSE:DXC) Q4 CY2025 Earnings Results: Revenue In Line With Expectations
feed.summary: DXC Technology met Wall Street's revenue expectations for Q4 CY2025 with sales flat year-on-year at $3.19 billion, though next quarter's guidance of $3.18 billion fell slightly below estimates. The company reported a non-GAAP profit of $0.96 per share, exceeding analyst consensus by 16.2%. Despite strong free cash flow and improved bookings, DXC faces ongoing challenges in revenue growth, with organic revenue declining and future projections indicating continued, albeit slower, sales shrinkage.

feed.title: Amazon Targeting $50 Billion For OpenAI Investment
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly considering a significant $50 billion investment in OpenAI, which would make it the largest contributor to OpenAI's latest funding round if finalized. This potential investment is part of OpenAI's efforts to raise substantial capital for expansion, including data center capacity, with other major players like Nvidia and Microsoft also contemplating large contributions. The discussions are still in early stages, but aim to close the funding round in the first quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Ford names a new head to its energy business startup
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has appointed Lisa Drake as president of its new business segment, Ford Energy, which will focus on producing battery energy storage systems in addition to EV batteries. Drake, previously vice president of Ford's Technology Platform Programs and EV Systems, will oversee the conversion of the Kentucky BlueOval SK Battery Park to produce utility-scale battery systems, with a goal of deploying at least 20 GWh annually by late 2027. The Michigan BlueOval Battery Park will produce smaller cells for residential energy storage.

feed.title: ATA Creativity Global raises $8.85M, ends Baby Capital talks
feed.summary: ATA Creativity Global (NASDAQ:AACG) announced the closing of a registered direct offering on January 28, 2026, raising approximately $8.85 million in gross proceeds by issuing 11,067,547 ADSs at $0.80 per ADS. The company plans to use the net proceeds for general working capital. Additionally, discussions with Baby BTC Strategic Capital for a separate potential transaction have been terminated.

feed.title: FRST: Returned to profitability in Q4 2025 with robust growth, improved efficiency, and higher capital
feed.summary: Primis Financial Corp. (FRST) returned to profitability in Q4 2025 with a net income of $30 million. The company reported strong growth in loans, deposits, and tangible book value, alongside a significant increase in noninterest income due to a major sale-leaseback gain. Additionally, FRST improved its efficiency and capital ratios.

feed.title: Valley National Bancorp Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Valley National Bancorp (NASDAQ: VLY) reported record Q4 2025 earnings and provided a positive outlook for 2026, anticipating continued profitability improvements. Key drivers include disciplined balance sheet management, growth in core deposits, and expanding net interest margin. The company expects mid-single-digit loan growth, 11-13% net interest income growth, and normalizing credit costs in the upcoming year.

feed.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT Declares February 2026 Monthly Dividend
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT (ARR) has announced a monthly cash dividend of $0.24 per share for February 2026, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026. As a REIT, the company is required to distribute most of its taxable income to maintain its tax status, and dividend levels are determined by the board based on various factors. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates ARR as Neutral, citing improving financial performance and a technical uptrend, but notes concerns regarding valuation and macro-uncertainty risks.

feed.title: Frost Bank continues to post strong returns from ongoing branch expansion in Texas
feed.summary: Frost Bank, led by Chairman and CEO Phil Green, continues to prioritize internal growth through branch expansion in Texas markets like Houston, Dallas, and Austin, rather than pursuing mergers or acquisitions. This strategy has proven successful, with the bank reporting strong net income and revenue growth in the fourth quarter and full year of 2025, exceeding analyst estimates. Since 2018, its branch expansion has led to a significant increase in financial centers, loans, and new customer households.

feed.title: LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin Corp (LMT) has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, highlighting significant financial and operational achievements, strategic initiatives, and challenges in the defense industry. The report details a 6% increase in sales to $75,048 million, primarily driven by strong performance in its Missiles and Fire Control, Aeronautics, and Space segments. Despite an increase in profits, net earnings and diluted EPS saw a slight decrease due to higher interest expenses and pension costs, while the company continues to expand production capacity, especially for the F-35 program.

feed.title: Jacobs increases quarterly dividend to $0.36 per share
feed.summary: Jacobs Solutions Inc. has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.36 per share, up from $0.32. The dividend is payable on March 20, 2026, to shareholders who are of record as of February 20, 2026. This news was published on January 29, 2026.

feed.title: ResMed (NYSE:RMD) Exceeds Q4 CY2025 Expectations
feed.summary: ResMed (NYSE:RMD) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, surpassing Wall Street's revenue and adjusted EPS expectations. The company's revenue grew 11% year-on-year to $1.42 billion, and non-GAAP profit reached $2.81 per share, exceeding analyst estimates. ResMed attributes its success to the strength of its global business and its commitment to advancing solutions for sleep and respiratory disorders.

feed.title: Gartner Crowns Salesforce Data 360 as CDP Leader — But the Real Story Is the Category Shift
feed.summary: Gartner's 2026 Magic Quadrant for Customer Data Platforms positions Salesforce Data 360 as a definitive leader, signaling a significant shift in the CDP market. The firm now views CDPs as "context engines" and foundational for enterprise AI, moving beyond mere marketing data unification. This "platformization and agentification" reflects a broader trend where successful AI implementation relies on unified, governed data architectures rather than just advanced models.

feed.title: HALPER SADEH LLC ENCOURAGES CUMMINS INC. SHAREHOLDERS TO CONTACT THE FIRM TO DISCUSS THEIR RIGHTS
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating whether certain officers and directors of Cummins Inc. (NYSE: CMI) breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages long-term Cummins shareholders to contact them to discuss potential legal rights and options, including seeking corporate governance reforms or financial relief. Halper Sadeh LLC handles these actions on a contingent fee basis, emphasizing that shareholder involvement can enhance corporate policies and shareholder value.

feed.title: Seacoast Banking: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Seacoast Banking Corp. of Florida (SBCF) reported fourth-quarter net income of $34.3 million, or 31 cents per share. Adjusted earnings of 44 cents per share fell short of analyst expectations of 51 cents per share. The company's revenue net of interest expense was $203.3 million, exceeding Street forecasts.

feed.title: Axos Financial earnings beat by $0.18, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Axos Financial (NYSE: AX) reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations for both earnings per share (EPS) and revenue. The company posted an EPS of $2.25, significantly higher than the estimated $2.07, and revenue of $385.09M, surpassing the consensus of $347.25M. The stock has shown solid performance, rising 21.51% in the last three months and 36.08% over the past year.

feed.title: Fidelity National Information Services Raises Quarterly Dividend to $0.44 per Share From $0.40, payable March 24 to Holders of Record March 10
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.44 per share, up from $0.40. The dividend is payable on March 24 to shareholders of record as of March 10. This news was published on MarketScreener and reported by MT Newswires.

feed.title: The AES Corp Options Spot-On: On January 29th, 146.24K Contracts Were Traded, With 225.44K Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 29th, The AES Corp (AES.US) experienced active options trading, with a total volume of 146.24K contracts, overwhelmingly dominated by call options (98.64%). The open interest for AES reached approximately 225.44K contracts, representing 78.02% of the past 30-day average. A notable trade involved a call option with a $12.00 strike price expiring in February 2026, which recorded 28,190 contracts and a turnover of $9.02 million.

feed.title: Gartner Adds AI and Investment Experts to Board
feed.summary: Gartner (IT) has appointed MIT artificial intelligence expert Professor Daniela Rus and veteran investment executive Edward Bousa to its Board of Directors, expanding the board to 13 members. These appointments aim to strengthen Gartner's focus on AI and investor-oriented value creation. The most recent analyst rating on IT stock is a Hold with a $275.00 price target.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp Issues $2 Billion Senior Notes
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp issued $2 billion in senior unsecured debt on January 29, 2026, consisting of fixed-to-floating rate senior notes due in 2032 and 2037. This offering generated approximately $1.99 billion in net proceeds, enhancing the bank's long-term funding flexibility and access to capital markets. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates FITB as "Outperform" due to solid financial performance and an anticipated uplift from the Comerica merger, despite some negative revenue and free cash flow growth signals.
